Apple Apple scab.cause: Fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis.
Apple Apple scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown spots on leaves, leaves may curl and drop prematurely.
Apple Apple scab.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply appropriate fungicide. Consult an agricultural expert.
Apple Apple scab.prevention: Plant resistant varieties and apply dormant sprays in early spring.
Apple Apple scab.disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.

Apple Black rot.cause: Fungal disease caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa.
Apple Black rot.symptoms: Brown leaf spots with purple borders and rotting fruit.
Apple Black rot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and consult an agricultural officer before applying fungicide.
Apple Black rot.prevention: Prune trees properly and maintain orchard hygiene.
Apple Black rot.disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.

Apple Cedar apple rust.cause: Fungal disease caused by Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae.
Apple Cedar apple rust.symptoms: Bright yellow-orange spots on leaves, forming cup-like structures.
Apple Cedar apple rust.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply fungicide. Consult an agricultural expert.
Apple Cedar apple rust.prevention: Avoid planting near juniper trees and use resistant varieties.
Apple Cedar apple rust.disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.

Coffee Rust.cause: Fungal disease caused by Hemileia vastatrix.
Coffee Rust.symptoms: Yellow-orange powdery spots on the underside of leaves leading to defoliation.
Coffee Rust.treatment: Apply fungicides and remove severely infected leaves. Consult an agricultural expert.
Coffee Rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties and maintain proper shade management.
Coffee Rust.disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.

Corn (maize) Cercospora leaf spot Gray l.cause: Fungal disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis.
Corn (maize) Cercospora leaf spot Gray l.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions on leaves, reducing photosynthetic area.
Corn (maize) Cercospora leaf spot Gray l.treatment: Apply fungicide at early symptom stage. Consult an agricultural expert.
Corn (maize) Cercospora leaf spot Gray l.prevention: Use resistant hybrids and practice crop rotation with non-host crops.

Grape Black rot.cause: Fungal disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii.
Grape Black rot.symptoms: Circular brown spots on leaves with black margins, fruit rot.
Grape Black rot.treatment: Remove infected plant parts and apply fungicide. Consult an agricultural expert.
Grape Black rot.prevention: Prune for air circulation and apply preventive fungicides during wet weather.

Grape Esca (Black Measles).cause: Fungal disease complex caused by Phaeomoniella chlamydospora and others.
Grape Esca (Black Measles).symptoms: Reddish-brown leaf spots with dark margins, streaking on stems.
Grape Esca (Black Measles).treatment: No effective cure. Remove infected vines and replant. Consult an agricultural expert.
Grape Esca (Black Measles).prevention: Use certified disease-free planting material and avoid wounding vines.
Grape Esca (Black Measles).disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.

Grape Leaf blight (Isariopsis Leaf Spot).treatment: Apply fungicide and remove infected leaves. Consult an agricultural expert.
Grape Leaf blight (Isariopsis Leaf Spot).prevention: Ensure good air circulation and practice proper vineyard sanitation.

Orange Haunglongbing (Citrus greening).cause: Bacterial disease caused by Candidatus Liberibacter spp., spread by psyllids.
Orange Haunglongbing (Citrus greening).symptoms: Yellow mottling on leaves, misshapen bitter fruit, premature fruit drop.
Orange Haunglongbing (Citrus greening).treatment: No cure. Remove infected trees immediately to prevent spread. Consult an agricultural expert.
Orange Haunglongbing (Citrus greening).prevention: Control psyllid populations with insecticides and use certified disease-free seedlings.
Orange Haunglongbing (Citrus greening).disclaimer: This is general guidance only. Consult an agricultural expert before treatment.
